title: Ish-Bel Tur
content:

|death=c. [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]], edge of [[Thaereian system|Thaereian space]]<ref name="20 BBY">The ''[[Living Force Campaign Guide]]'' states that the [[Living Force (roleplaying campaign)|''Living Force'']] campaign is set [[31 BBY/Legends|one year]] after the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. As the ''Living Force'' adventure ''[[Below the Belt: Philology|Philology]]'' [[Cularin's disappearance|moved]] the campaign forward [[21 BBY/Legends|ten years]] to the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], and the adventure ''[[Night Eyes: Night's Homecoming|Night's Homecoming]]'' established that by Year 4 of the campaign over a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] had elapsed since the events of ''Philology'', "[[Thaere Is Not Your Friend]]," which details the [[Death/Legends|death]] of Ish-Bel Tur at the edge of [[Thaereian system|Thaereian space]], can therefore be dated to circa [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]].</ref>

{{Quote|This morning, the Thaereian ship transporting Gerta Haman to Coruscant, where she was scheduled to meet with Senator Wren to discuss the allegations made on this program at the request of the Senator, was attacked. Haman and fourteen members of the Thaereian Navy were on board. Jedi Knight Ish-Bel Tur served as guardian of the mission and liaison from the Almas Academy. Three minutes after the initial distress call, the ship exploded at the edge of Thaereian space. There were no survivors. The Thaereian government has offered a sizable reward…|Ryk Osentay, broadcasting on "Eye on Cularin"|<ref name="TINYF" />}}
'''Ish-Bel Tur''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] at the [[Almas Academy]], a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jedi academy|training institution]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Almas]], during the final [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Around [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]], after the activist [[Gerta Haman]] was arrested for denouncing the [[Thaereian military]], which the Republic had [[Thaereian Navy charter|tasked]] with defending Almas and the rest of the [[Cularin system/Legends|Cularin system]], Tur was assigned to accompany a [[Thaereian]] [[Navy/Legends|naval]] [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] that was transporting Haman to the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. However, the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] exploded en route on the edge of [[Thaereian system|Thaereian space]], and Tur and Haman were [[Death/Legends|killed]]. Tur's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] was later seized as a trophy by the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Wampa/Legends|wampa]] [[Ku-Kak]] and wielded by the [[Anzat (species)|Anzat]] [[Force Adept]] [[Mischa Vorfren]].

==Biography==
===Service as a Jedi===
Ish-Bel Tur was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] individual<ref name="AMA" /> who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the final [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Tur was based at the [[Almas Academy]], a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] [[Jedi academy|facility]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Almas]] in the [[Cularin system/Legends|Cularin system]], and he [[Jedi Trials/Legends|attained]] the rank of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]].<ref name="TINYF" /> He also constructed for himself a [[Color/Legends|green]]-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].<ref name="AMA" />

In about [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]],<ref name="20 BBY" /> during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the activist [[Gerta Haman]] was interviewed by the [[Journalist/Legends|journalist]] [[Ryk Osentay]] for the news feature "[[Eye on Cularin]]" about a recent [[TINYF|popular campaign]] in the Cularin [[Star system/Legends|system]] to denounce the [[Thaereian military]],<ref name="TINYF" /> an [[Army/Legends|armed force]] from the [[Thaereian system]] that the Republic had [[Thaereian Navy charter|tasked]] with maintaining security on [[Cularin/Legends|Cularin]].<ref name="OT">{{Wizards|url=rpga/livingforce/article.asp?x=lf/swlf20011004b_thaere&c=swlfc|text=Over Thaere}}</ref> However, Haman was arrested<ref name="TINYF" /> by the [[Thaereian military police]]<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|11|Haman, Gerta}}</ref> during the broadcast after she claimed that some senior [[Thaereian]] [[Officer/Legends|officers]] had received a payment of 200 million [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] from the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan/Legends|InterGalactic Banking Clan]], which was allied with the Republic's opponent in the Clone Wars, the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]]. The Thaereians agreed to transport Haman to the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to meet with [[Lavina Durada-Vashne Wren|Lavina Wren]], the [[Senator/Legends|senator]] for Cularin, to discuss Haman's accusations, and Tur was assigned by the Jedi to accompany Haman, both as a guardian and as a representative of the Almas [[School|Academy]].<ref name="TINYF" />

En route to Coruscant, the Thaereian [[Navy/Legends|naval]] [[Starship/Legends|ship]] carrying Tur and Haman<ref name="TINYF" /> fell under attack<ref name="CSWE" /> on the edge of Thaereian space. The vessel sent out a [[Distress signal/Legends|distress signal]], but it exploded three [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]] later, resulting in the [[Death/Legends|deaths]] of Tur, Haman, and the entire Thaereian crew. Following the explosion, the [[Thaereian Government]] offered a sizable reward for information on the cause of the ship's destruction. Shortly afterward, Ryk Osentay broadcast news of the explosion and the demise of Tur and Haman on an edition of "Eye on Cularin."<ref name="TINYF" />

===Legacy===
Tur's lightsaber was later seized as a trophy by [[Ku-Kak]], a [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-sensitive [[Wampa/Legends|wampa]] who served as a [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] of the [[Slaver/Legends|slaver]] [[Phylus Mon]]. The [[Anzat (species)|Anzat]] [[Force Adept]] [[Mischa Vorfren]], another of Mon's minions, took the weapon as his own and learned how to fight with it.<ref name="AMA" />

{{RPG|''[[A Mon Alone]]''}}
Mon and his minions later ventured to the Cularin system on a mission to break into the [[Almas Sith fortress|Sith fortress]] on Almas and recover the secrets of the creation of [[Sith battlelord]]s, a class of [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] developed by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Rivan]]. However, Mon and his associates were [[Skirmish aboard the Animiasma|defeated]] aboard Mon's vessel ''[[Animiasma]]'' by a group of freelance agents who operated in the system known as the "[[Heroes of Cularin]]," and the agents recovered Ish-Bel Tur's lightsaber from Vorfren.<ref name="AMA" />
{{Endgame}}

==Equipment==
Ish-Bel Tur wielded a green-bladed lightsaber that was marked on the [[Hilt/Legends|hilt]] with his own personal emblem.<ref name="AMA" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ish-Bel Tur was first mentioned in "[[Thaere Is Not Your Friend]]," a short story written by [[Morrie Mullins (real person)|Morrie Mullins]] and published on [[Wizards.com]] in [[November]] [[2003]] as a tie-in to ''[[Night Eyes: Night's Homecoming|Night's Homecoming]]'', a [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|roleplaying]] supplement in the [[Living Force (roleplaying campaign)|''Living Force'']] campaign.<ref name="TINYF" /> Tur received a further mention in the [[2004]] ''Living Force'' module ''[[A Mon Alone]]'', also by Mullins.<ref name="AMA" /> Neither source specifies Tur's [[Species/Legends|species]] or [[Gender/Legends|gender]].<ref name="TINYF" /><ref name="AMA" />

In ''A Mon Alone'', the players [[Roleplaying game|roleplay]] as the Heroes of Cularin, and it is possible for them to obtain Tur's lightsaber during their battle against Phylus Mon and his minions, either by looting it or by being given it willingly by Mischa Vorfren in return for ionizing a small explosive that Mon had placed in the Force Adept's [[Heart/Legends|heart]] in order to be able to control him. If any of the player characters are Jedi, then they have the option to petition the [[Jedi Master]]s [[Lanius Qel-Bertuk]] and [[Devan For'deschel]] for permission to keep the weapon as a tribute to Tur, or otherwise it must be returned to the Almas Academy for use in training Jedi [[Padawan]]s.<ref name="AMA" />

Though it did not mention Ish-Bel Tur, the [[2008]] compendium ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' introduced a minor contradiction regarding Tur's final moments. Whereas "Thaere Is Not Your Friend" states that the Thaereian vessel that was carrying Tur and Gerta Haman at the time of their deaths was traveling to Coruscant,<ref name="TINYF" /> Shaman's entry in the encyclopedia states the ship was en route to the planet [[Thaere Privo]] when it was destroyed.<ref name="CSWE" />
